headline: Week four: deload landed just under target, calf quiet
narrative: Deload week, and for once coming in a whisker under target is exactly the mark of success. After Finland the ask was to absorb the work, and the shape of the week mostly fits: five days of genuine nothing on the training ledger beyond easy walks — including a lunch stop to hand-feed red squirrels — with a gym session and a short row keeping the engine warm. Saturday was a parkrun celebrating a clubmate's thirtieth, sensibly flat and short.

The one honest caveat: nearly all the week's vert came in a single Sunday outing over Win Hill, Crook Hill and Bamford Moor — a proper Peaks run, and a bit more of a day out than a recovery week strictly wants, even if the totals stayed inside the line. The calf had nothing to say all week, which makes it four weeks running with no complaint worse than the opening twinge. Rested and roughly on plan at the quarter mark of Base, the build resumes now: week five doubles the ask, with steeper climbs and a deliberate downhill eccentric focus. Fresh legs — go use them.
